summary: Key enzymes in calcification include alkaline phosphatase and pyrophosphatase; these differ from hormone-based regulators of calcium levels such as parathyroid hormone and calcitonin. Alkaline phosphatase plays a crucial role during bone formation by facilitating mineral deposition into osteoid tissue while pyrophosphatases help regulate extracellular matrix minerals preventing excessive precipitation leading to pathological conditions if dysregulated or deficient respectively.
